explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，意思是「環顧、四下張望」。
generatedExample: He looked round the station for a taxi.

explanationZhTw: shabby 表示「破舊的、寒酸的」，常用來形容外觀老舊或不整潔。
generatedExample: They rented a shabby little room near the harbor.

explanationZhTw: enormous 表示「巨大的、非常大的」。
generatedExample: An enormous bed filled most of the small bedroom.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「編造」，而是指床「鋪好、整理好」了。
generatedExample: The guest bed was made up before everyone arrived.

explanationZhTw: coverless 表示「沒有套子或外罩的」。
generatedExample: He slept on a coverless pillow after the wash.

explanationZhTw: bolster 是一種「長枕頭、抱枕式枕頭」。
generatedExample: She placed a bolster behind her back for support.

explanationZhTw: mantelpiece 指「壁爐上方的架子或台面」。
generatedExample: A photo frame sat on the mantelpiece.

explanationZhTw: gateleg 指一種「折疊式桌子」，桌腳可像門板一樣翻開支撐桌面。
generatedExample: They unfolded the gateleg table for dinner.

explanationZhTw: half-darkness 指「半黑半暗、昏暗」的狀態，強調光線很弱。
generatedExample: We could barely see each other in the half-darkness.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `fender` 不是汽車的葉子板，而是壁爐前的金屬護欄或爐座周邊。屬於依上下文才知道的特殊用法。
generatedExample: He placed the tools beside the fender before lighting the fire.

explanationZhTw: `battered` 表示「破舊的、受損的」，常用來描述被長期使用或碰撞後變得很舊的東西。
generatedExample: The battered chair still had one strong leg.

explanationZhTw: `saucepan` 是「平底深鍋、湯鍋」的意思，屬於常見廚房器具名稱。
generatedExample: She warmed the soup in a small saucepan.

explanationZhTw: `burner` 在這裡是指爐具上的「爐頭、燃燒器」，不是一般動詞 `burn` 的延伸意思。
generatedExample: He turned the burner down after the water started boiling.

explanationZhTw: `saccharine tablets` 指「糖精錠」，是人工甜味劑。`saccharine` 在這裡不是形容詞「過甜的、做作的」，而是名詞用法。
generatedExample: She dropped two saccharine tablets into her tea.

explanationZhTw: `clock's hands` 是鐘錶的「指針」。這裡的 `hands` 不是「手」，而是依鐘面上下文產生的特殊意思。
generatedExample: The clock's hands pointed to half past eight.

explanationZhTw: 「gratuitous」在這裡是「無端的、沒有必要的」；整體是在說這種愚蠢是明知故犯、毫無必要，卻還去做的。
generatedExample: His apology felt gratuitous because nobody had blamed him.

explanationZhTw: 「floated into his head」是慣用說法，表示想法突然冒出來、自然浮現腦中。
generatedExample: The solution floated into her head while she was on the bus.

explanationZhTw: 「made no difficulty about」表示「沒有為難、很爽快地接受或同意」。這裡是指他很順利就答應把房間租出去。
generatedExample: The manager made no difficulty about changing my shift.

explanationZhTw: 「looked into the middle distance」是文學式表達，指他沒有正眼看人，而是望向遠處，帶有若有所思、刻意保持距離的感覺。
generatedExample: When asked about the mistake, he looked into the middle distance before answering.

explanationZhTw: 「spoke in generalities」表示說話很籠統，不講具體細節。這裡是在寫他故意避開敏感話題。
generatedExample: During the interview, she spoke in generalities and never gave a direct answer.

explanationZhTw: 「gave on」是動詞片語，意思是「通向、面向」。這裡指後院通到一條巷子。
generatedExample: Our hotel room gave on a quiet garden.

explanationZhTw: `peeped out` 是片語動詞，意思是「偷偷往外看、探出頭看」。這裡帶有小心觀察外面情況的感覺。
generatedExample: She peeped out from behind the door to see who was there.

explanationZhTw: `court` 在這裡不是「法院」，而是指建築物中間的「天井、院子、庭院」。屬於依上下文才看得出的特殊意思。
generatedExample: The children were playing in the court behind the apartment block.

explanationZhTw: `monstrous` 這裡不是字面上的「怪物般的」，而是誇張地形容這位女人身形巨大、非常壯碩。
generatedExample: He was a monstrous man with shoulders like a wall.

explanationZhTw: `solid as a Norman pillar` 是比喻／慣用說法，表示「非常結實、穩固、紮實」。這裡用來強調那位女人像柱子一樣壯實。
generatedExample: The old bridge stood solid as a Norman pillar after the storm.

explanationZhTw: `to and fro` 是慣用語，表示「來回地、往返地」。這裡描述她在兩個地方之間反覆走動。
generatedExample: The cat paced to and fro across the kitchen floor.

explanationZhTw: `pegging out` 是片語動詞，意思是「用衣夾把東西夾在繩子上晾乾」。這裡指把尿布晾在晾衣繩上。
generatedExample: She spent the morning pegging out the laundry in the garden.

explanationZhTw: `contralto` 是樂理／聲音術語，指「女低音」或非常低沉的女性歌聲。這不是日常高頻字，但在描述聲音時很精確。
generatedExample: Her contralto gave the song a deep, rich sound.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 fancy 不是「幻想」那麼簡單，而是指一時的迷戀、愛慕；整句是在說那只是一場沒結果的單戀或癡心妄想。
generatedExample: At first I thought it was love, but it turned out to be only a fancy.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是在比喻那段感情很快就消逝了；Ipril dye 是帶有方言／拼字變形的寫法，對應到常見的 "April day" 之類表達，重點是「轉瞬即逝」。
generatedExample: Their excitement passed like a spring day and was gone before noon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 stirred 不是字面上的「攪動」，而是指「喚起、激起」了夢想、情感或想像。
generatedExample: Her message stirred old memories in me.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「偷走我的心」，也就是讓我墜入愛河、完全被對方吸引。
generatedExample: His smile stole my heart away almost immediately.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是字面上的「鬼魂出沒」，而是指這首曲子持續在倫敦各處縈繞、反覆出現，讓人印象深刻。
generatedExample: That melody kept haunting my head all afternoon.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語表示「為了某人而製作／讓某人受益、享用」，不是字面上的「利益」。
generatedExample: The guide was written for the benefit of new employees.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「人為介入、人工參與」，強調歌詞不是由人直接寫出來的。
generatedExample: The report was generated without human intervention.

explanationZhTw: 這是小說中的虛構機器名稱，指可以自動寫詩或編歌詞的裝置。
generatedExample: In the story, a versificator could produce rhymes in seconds.

explanationZhTw: 表示「唱得很悅耳、很有旋律感」，是一種描述聲音優美的副詞。
generatedExample: She sang tunefully despite being nervous.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 rubbish 不是一般垃圾，而是指「很糟的東西、拙劣的作品」；dreadful 加強貶義。
generatedExample: His first draft was dreadful rubbish, but the final version was strong.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「好奇地」，而是「奇怪地、出乎意料地」安靜。
generatedExample: Curiously, nobody mentioned the missing file.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語表示「多虧了、由於」，通常說明某件事造成的原因，而且語氣可正可負。
generatedExample: Thanks to your help, we finished on time.

explanationZhTw: 這是小說中的虛構裝置，像是可監視、可播放內容的螢幕，帶有反烏托邦世界觀。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room never switched off.

explanationZhTw: 形容某事幾乎無法想像、難以相信，語氣比「不太可能」更強。
generatedExample: It seemed inconceivable that the small team could finish the whole project in one day.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 frequent 是動詞，意思是「經常去、常出入」，不是常見的形容詞用法。
generatedExample: Students often frequent the library during exam week.

explanationZhTw: 指「誘惑、吸引力」，表示某事讓人很想去做。
generatedExample: The temptation to check my phone was hard to resist during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 指「藏身處、隱蔽的地方」，可用來避開別人或躲藏。
generatedExample: The children built a hiding-place under the stairs.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示「讓兩人都無法抗拒」或「對兩人來說太有吸引力了」。
generatedExample: The offer had been too much for both of them, so they accepted immediately.

explanationZhTw: 片語，表示「為了預期中的事情、為了迎接即將到來的情況」。
generatedExample: We stocked extra supplies in anticipation of the storm.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「需要、牽涉到」，強調某事所包含的工作或結果。
generatedExample: The move entailed a lot of paperwork and planning.

explanationZhTw: 這裡 secure 是「設法取得、成功安排到」的意思，不是「保護」或「固定」。
generatedExample: I finally secured a meeting room for the presentation.

explanationZhTw: drifted towards 表示「不自覺地、慢慢地朝某方向移動」，帶有輕微、自然的動作感。
generatedExample: The two friends drifted towards the window to watch the rain.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `off` 不是「離開」的意思，而是表示「取消了、作廢了、沒戲了」。整句是在說事情不再照原本計畫進行。
generatedExample: The picnic is all off because of the storm.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `safe` 是語境義，意思是「不會有風險、可以放心做」；不是單純指身體上的安全。
generatedExample: When the teacher left, we thought it was safe to talk.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `come` 不是單純的「來」，而是表示「到場、出席、過來」；整句 `can't come` 就是「不能來／不能出席」的意思。
generatedExample: I have a meeting tomorrow, so I can't come to dinner.

explanationZhTw: 常用來表示同意、接受建議或反問「為什麼不呢？」；語氣通常很自然，等於「好啊」「何不呢」。
generatedExample: “Do you want to join us for dinner?” “Why not?”

explanationZhTw: `usual` 表示「平常的、慣常的、通常會出現的」。這裡是在說「那個老是會有的理由／一貫的理由」。
generatedExample: That's the usual answer when he doesn't want to explain.

explanationZhTw: 指「發生性關係／做愛」；這裡是委婉說法，不是字面上的「製造愛」。
generatedExample: They spoke about their love-making with unusual honesty.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「生理需要」，而是說他把她視為近乎不可或缺的存在。
generatedExample: After weeks of hiking, clean water became a physical necessity.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 cheat 不是「作弊」或「詐騙」，而是指他覺得自己被辜負、被虧待了。
generatedExample: He felt she was cheating him out of a fair chance.

explanationZhTw: 指「突然抓住／支配了他」，常用來形容情緒、想法或感覺強烈地湧上來。
generatedExample: A strange fear took hold of him during the interview.

explanationZhTw: 指「零零碎碎的小東西、雜貨、瑣碎物品」。
generatedExample: I picked up a few odds and ends at the market.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是委婉語，意思是「做愛／發生性關係」。
generatedExample: They wanted privacy so they could make love in peace.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「他突然想到、某個念頭浮現在他腦中」。
generatedExample: It occurred to him that he had left his keys at home.

explanationZhTw: 指「瘋狂、極不理智的舉動」；這裡不是醫學診斷，而是強烈表示這件事很蠢、很危險。
generatedExample: Buying a car you cannot afford would be pure lunacy.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「走近自己的墳墓」，比喻在主動走向死亡、毀滅或嚴重後果。
generatedExample: Ignoring the warning signs felt like stepping nearer to his grave.

explanationZhTw: 指「命中注定的、早已安排好的」；帶有宿命感。
generatedExample: They spoke about the accident as if it were predestined.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「延後、推遲」某事發生。
generatedExample: We may need to postpone the meeting until Monday.

explanationZhTw: 指「故意的、有意為之的」；這裡強調明知後果仍刻意去做。
generatedExample: It was a wilful decision, not a mistake.

explanationZhTw: 「burst into」在這裡是片語動詞，表示突然闖入或衝進來，不是字面上的「爆裂進入」。
generatedExample: She burst into the kitchen with an urgent message.

explanationZhTw: 「coarse」在這裡指材質粗糙、質地不細緻，重點是外觀和手感。
generatedExample: The shirt felt coarse against my skin.

explanationZhTw: 「canvas」是帆布，一種厚實耐用的布料。
generatedExample: He packed the gear into a sturdy canvas bag.

explanationZhTw: 「to and fro」是慣用語，意思是來回、往返地移動。
generatedExample: The children ran to and fro across the yard.

explanationZhTw: 「disengaged herself」在這裡不是抽象的「解除連結」，而是指她把自己從他的擁抱或接近中抽開。
generatedExample: She disengaged herself from the crowd and walked away.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「骯髒」，而是帶有貶抑語氣，表示這咖啡很差、很難喝。
generatedExample: The soup was filthy, so nobody wanted a second bowl.

explanationZhTw: 口語片語動詞，意思是把某物丟掉、扔掉。
generatedExample: If the note is wrong, just chuck it away.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `be needing` 是英式口語／偏老派的未來說法，意思是「不會需要了」。
generatedExample: We shan't be needing coats if the weather stays warm.

explanationZhTw: 這是用來引起對方注意的說法，意思接近「聽我說」或「你看」。
generatedExample: Look here, we need to finish this now.

explanationZhTw: 表示「跪下」；這裡是在描述她立刻雙膝著地的動作。
generatedExample: She fell on her knees to pick up the broken glass.

explanationZhTw: 表示「猛地打開」或「用力掀開」，帶有突然、粗魯的動作感。
generatedExample: He threw open the window to let in fresh air.

explanationZhTw: 表示「滾出來、散落出來」；這裡指工具從袋子裡一股腦掉出來。
generatedExample: The books tumbled out of the box when I lifted it.

explanationZhTw: 表示「一些、若干」；是比單純說 many 更中性的數量表達。
generatedExample: There were a number of people waiting outside the store.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是常見的「產生、屈服」，而是指「在壓力下讓開、凹陷、變形」。
generatedExample: The ground yielded slightly under my feet.

explanationZhTw: 「saccharine」在這裡是指人工甜味劑糖精，不是日常最基本的「糖」。這是較書面、較少見的詞，了解它有助於看出角色在強調「真的糖」和替代品的差別。
generatedExample: I prefer real sugar to saccharine in tea.

explanationZhTw: 「bloody」是英式口語中的強調語，帶有抱怨或不滿的語氣，這裡不是字面上的「血腥」，而是等於「該死的／破爛的」這種情緒性用法。
generatedExample: This bloody machine keeps breaking down.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「tin」是英式英語常見用法，意思接近「罐頭」或「金屬罐」，不是字面上的錫金屬本身。
generatedExample: She opened a tin of soup for lunch.

explanationZhTw: 「sacking」指粗麻布、麻袋布這類材質，用來包裹東西。這個詞在一般口語中不算最常見，但在這裡很具體地描寫了包裝材料。
generatedExample: He covered the box with sacking to protect it.

explanationZhTw: 把它包起來；這裡是字面意思，指用紙或布把東西包好。
generatedExample: She wrapped it up in newspaper to keep it safe.

explanationZhTw: 散發出的氣息或氣味；這裡是比較文學性的用法，指氣味像是從過去的記憶中散出來。
generatedExample: The faint emanation of smoke drifted from the alley.

explanationZhTw: 偶爾遇到、碰上；這裡表示那種氣味在某些情況下仍可能聞到。
generatedExample: You may still meet with this issue after the update.

explanationZhTw: 「murmured」表示「低聲說、喃喃地說」，通常音量很小，帶有私下或輕聲的語氣。
generatedExample: She murmured an apology before leaving the room.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的專有名詞，指黨內最高階、最核心的統治階層；不是一般「內部政黨」的字面意思。
generatedExample: The Inner Party controls the most sensitive decisions in the state.

explanationZhTw: `manage to` 在這裡不是「管理」，而是「設法成功做成某事」。整句是在問對方怎麼有辦法弄到這些東西。
generatedExample: I managed to finish the report before the deadline.

explanationZhTw: `get hold of` 是常見片語，表示「拿到、取得、弄到手」。這裡帶有一點「怎麼弄到這些東西的」的意思。
generatedExample: Where did you get hold of that old book?

explanationZhTw: 在《1984》中，Inner Party 指統治核心的菁英階層。
generatedExample: The Inner Party controls the most important decisions.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 stuff 不是「事情」，而是泛指「東西、物品」。
generatedExample: I left my stuff by the door.

explanationZhTw: swine 在這裡是貶抑用法，意思是「卑鄙的人、討厭的傢伙」，不是字面上的豬。
generatedExample: Those swine lied to everyone.

explanationZhTw: pinch things 是英式口語，意思是「偷東西」。
generatedExample: Someone pinched my umbrella at the station.

explanationZhTw: packet 這裡是「小包、小包裝」的意思。
generatedExample: She opened a packet of biscuits.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「蹲下、蹲坐在地上」。不是一般站著或坐著，而是把身體壓低到蹲姿。
generatedExample: He squatted down to look under the table.

explanationZhTw: 指「用力撕開」。`tear open` 常用來表示把袋子、包裝或信封快速撕破。
generatedExample: She tore open the envelope and read the note.

explanationZhTw: `packet` 在這裡是「小包裝、小袋子」的意思，常指裝著食物、粉末或小物件的包。
generatedExample: He opened the packet of biscuits and shared them.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `real` 不是「真實的」抽象意思，而是「真正的、不是假貨或替代品」；整個片語 `real tea` 指的是「真正的茶葉沖泡的茶」，和後面的 `blackberry leaves` 這種替代品形成對比。
generatedExample: This is real tea, not the instant stuff.

explanationZhTw: 「vaguely」表示「含糊地、模糊地、不太確定地」。這裡是在描述她講話時語氣不清楚，像是在隨口說、沒有把事情說明白。
generatedExample: She smiled vaguely and changed the subject.

explanationZhTw: 「turn your back on someone」一般可表示「背棄、拋棄某人」，但這裡是字面用法，意思是「把背轉向我、背對著我」。
generatedExample: Please turn your back on me while I change clothes.

explanationZhTw: 「turn round」是英式說法，意思是「轉過身、回頭」。這裡是直接命令，不是比喻用法。
generatedExample: He turned round when he heard his name.

explanationZhTw: 「心不在焉地、出神地」；表示注意力不在眼前的事物上。
generatedExample: She stared abstractedly out of the window during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「薄紗；細棉布」；這裡是指窗簾的布料。
generatedExample: The dress was made from light muslin that moved easily in the wind.

explanationZhTw: 「來回地」；固定片語，表示在兩個位置之間反覆往返。
generatedExample: He paced to and fro while waiting for the bus.

explanationZhTw: 「洗衣盆」；用來手洗衣物的大盆。
generatedExample: She filled the washtub with warm water and soap.

explanationZhTw: 「晾衣繩」；這裡不是一般的「線」，而是掛衣服晾乾的繩子。
generatedExample: The shirts were hanging on the line to dry.

explanationZhTw: 「衣夾」；這裡指夾衣服用的夾子，不是一般的木釘。
generatedExample: He clipped the towel to the line with two pegs.

explanationZhTw: 「很有感情地」；表示唱歌時投入強烈情緒。
generatedExample: The singer delivered the final verse with deep feeling.

explanationZhTw: 這是諺語，意思是「時間能沖淡痛苦、讓事情慢慢好轉」。
generatedExample: People say time heals all things, but some memories still take effort to face.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用說法，表示「深深觸動我的情感、讓我心裡很受影響」。
generatedExample: That old song still twists my heart-strings every time I hear it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「胡扯的、很蠢的」；不是字面上的流口水，而是在貶低那首歌內容很無聊。
generatedExample: He kept repeating drivelling excuses that nobody believed.

explanationZhTw: 表示「憑記憶、倒背如流」。
generatedExample: My sister knows the opening speech by heart.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「用衣夾把東西掛出去晾乾」。
generatedExample: She spent the morning pegging out wet clothes in the yard.

explanationZhTw: 表示「自發地、非事先安排地」。
generatedExample: The children started singing spontaneously after the show.

explanationZhTw: 指「不合常規的、非傳統的」。
generatedExample: Her unorthodox approach solved a problem that others had missed.

explanationZhTw: 指「古怪行為、怪癖」。
generatedExample: His eccentricity made him a memorable teacher.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純字面「飢餓程度」，而是指「接近挨餓的生活水平／生存邊緣」。
generatedExample: The family was living close to the starvation level after the factory closed.

explanationZhTw: 「turn round」在這裡是口語用法，意思是「轉過身／轉身」，也就是把身體朝相反方向轉過來。
generatedExample: She told him to turn round so she could fix the collar.

explanationZhTw: `turn round` 指「轉過身、回頭看」；這裡是描述他轉身後看見她。
generatedExample: She turned round when someone called her name.

explanationZhTw: `fail to recognize` 指「沒能認出」；這裡強調他一瞬間差點沒認出她。
generatedExample: I failed to recognize him at first because he had changed so much.

explanationZhTw: `actually` 在這裡不是「其實」的口語補充，而是用來強調真實情況或真實想法。
generatedExample: I actually thought the meeting started at nine.

explanationZhTw: `naked` 指「赤裸的、沒穿衣服的」。
generatedExample: The statue was carved with a naked figure.

explanationZhTw: `transformation` 指「徹底改變、變化」；這裡是指她外表或形象的明顯改變。
generatedExample: The transformation of the old factory into a museum took two years.

explanationZhTw: `painted her face` 在這裡不是字面上的「把臉塗上油漆」，而是指「化妝、塗臉妝」。
generatedExample: For the performance, she painted her face with bright colors.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，表示「溜進、悄悄進入」某個地方，帶有不太顯眼或很快進去的感覺。
generatedExample: He slipped into the room before anyone noticed him.

explanationZhTw: 「proletarian」指無產階級的、勞工階層的；整個片語是在說「工人階級居住的區域」。
generatedExample: They live in the proletarian quarters near the factory.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「化妝用品、化妝材料」，不是一般的「材料」，而是用來上妝的一整套東西。
generatedExample: She packed her make-up materials before the trip.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「抹上腮紅／胭脂」的意思，形容臉頰被化妝品染紅了。
generatedExample: Her cheeks were rouged lightly for the photo shoot.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「弄成粉末」，而是指臉上「撲粉、上蜜粉」的化妝動作。
generatedExample: Her face was powdered to reduce shine before the speech.

explanationZhTw: 「cosmetics」是化妝品；整句強調她臉上有化妝，和前文的世界觀形成反差。
generatedExample: She usually wears no cosmetics on her face.

explanationZhTw: 「dab」是少量輕點；這裡指只要幾點顏色、幾筆化妝，就能改變外觀。
generatedExample: A few dabs of colour made the painting look more lively.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，意思是「女性的、女性化的」，這裡是在說她看起來更有女人味。
generatedExample: The design has a soft, feminine style.

explanationZhTw: 「boyish」是像男孩般的；「overalls」是連身工作服。合起來是說她穿著帶點男孩子氣的工作服。
generatedExample: He wore boyish overalls and a plain shirt.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「synthetic」是人造的、合成的；「synthetic violets」指人造的紫羅蘭香味，而不是真花。
generatedExample: The room smelled like synthetic violets from cheap perfume.

explanationZhTw: 這是誇張的比喻，表示香味非常濃烈，一下子充滿了他的鼻子。
generatedExample: The smell of coffee flooded his nostrils when he entered the kitchen.

explanationZhTw: 「scent」在這裡是名詞，意思是「氣味、香味」。它不是在說抽象的概念，而是指可以聞到的味道，屬於常見但仍值得留意的字義。
generatedExample: I could smell the scent of coffee from the kitchen.

explanationZhTw: 「scent」在這裡是「香味、香水味」的意思；屬於常見字，但在這段裡可幫助理解人物在注意女性化打扮的細節。
generatedExample: The room had a soft scent of lavender.

explanationZhTw: 「get hold of」是片語動詞，意思是「弄到、取得、找到並拿到」。這裡表示她打算設法弄到一件真的女人洋裝。
generatedExample: I need to get hold of a charger before my phone dies.

explanationZhTw: 「frock」在這裡指「洋裝、連身裙」，帶有較舊式或文學色彩，不是最常見的日常說法。
generatedExample: She wore a blue frock to the party.

explanationZhTw: 「bloody」是英式口語裡常見的加強語氣字，帶有粗俗、不耐煩或強烈情緒色彩；這裡是在加重對「trousers」的厭惡感。
generatedExample: It's bloody cold outside today.

explanationZhTw: 「high-heeled shoes」指「高跟鞋」；在這裡是用來強化女性打扮的意象。
generatedExample: She bought a pair of high-heeled shoes for the wedding.

explanationZhTw: 「Party comrade」在這裡不是一般的「同伴」，而是帶有政治意味的稱呼，指「黨內同志／黨的成員」；屬於情境性用法。
generatedExample: He stopped speaking like a Party comrade and started talking like a person.

explanationZhTw: 「fling ... off」表示迅速、隨手把衣服脫掉；這裡帶有匆忙、隨性的動作感。
generatedExample: He flung his coat off and ran into the house.

explanationZhTw: 「meagre」指少量的、貧乏的，這裡形容身體瘦弱、單薄。
generatedExample: They survived on a meagre budget for months.

explanationZhTw: 「varicose veins」是靜脈曲張，指腿部等處鼓起、彎曲變粗的靜脈。
generatedExample: My grandmother has varicose veins in both legs.

explanationZhTw: 「threadbare」原本指布料磨損到快露出線頭，這裡形容毯子很舊、磨薄了。
generatedExample: The old threadbare sofa was still comfortable.

explanationZhTw: 「springiness」指彈性、回彈感；這裡特別在說床墊坐躺時的柔軟回彈。
generatedExample: I like the springiness of this mattress.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「bugs」不是一般籠統的「蟲」，而是指床上可能有臭蟲、床蝨等害蟲，是情境中的特殊意思。
generatedExample: The cabin looked clean, but it was full of bugs.

explanationZhTw: 「nowadays」表示「現在、如今」，用來對比過去與當下的情況。
generatedExample: Nowadays, many people work from home.

explanationZhTw: 「proles」是《1984》中的特定用語，指「無產階級／工人階級」；這是帶有政治語境的專門稱呼。
generatedExample: The proles lived in the poorest part of the city.

explanationZhTw: 「fell asleep」是「睡著了」的意思，屬於常見片語動詞。
generatedExample: I fell asleep on the train and missed my stop.

explanationZhTw: 「woke up」是「醒來」的意思，屬於常見片語動詞。
generatedExample: She woke up early because of the noise outside.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「crook」不是「騙子」，而是指手臂彎曲形成的內側彎處。
generatedExample: The baby fell asleep in the crook of her mother's arm.

explanationZhTw: 「make-up」在這裡是指「化妝品／妝容」。
generatedExample: She removed her make-up before going to bed.

explanationZhTw: 「bolster」在這裡是「長枕、靠枕」，不是一般的「支持」。
generatedExample: He leaned against a bolster while reading in bed.

explanationZhTw: 「rouge」是「胭脂、腮紅」，屬於較文學或舊式的化妝用語。
generatedExample: A touch of rouge made her cheeks look warmer.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「abolished」是「已被廢除、消失的」意思，和一般只表示「廢除」的用法相比，帶有回想過去制度已不存在的語感。
generatedExample: In the abolished system, people no longer had that old restriction.

explanationZhTw: 「compulsion」是「強迫、不得不做的壓力」，這裡指沒有非起床不可的強制感。
generatedExample: She felt no compulsion to answer immediately.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指水因為持續加熱而蒸發掉、變少了，不是單純「煮沸」的字面意思。
generatedExample: The sauce boiled away while I was answering the phone.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「起身；起床」。這裡是說她等一下要站起來去做咖啡。
generatedExample: I usually get up at seven on weekdays.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 cut ... off 表示「把燈關掉」，是口語說法，不是「切斷」的字面意思。
generatedExample: Please cut the lights off before you leave.

explanationZhTw: 英式英文中 flat 指「公寓、套房」。這裡不是「平坦的」，而是住所類型。
generatedExample: She lives in a small flat near the river.

explanationZhTw: 這是 24 小時制的時間說法，表示晚上 11:30。
generatedExample: The meeting starts at twenty-three thirty, so please arrive a little early.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `twenty-three` 不是單純的數字 23，而是指晚上 11 點，也就是 23:00。
generatedExample: The meeting starts at twenty-three, so don't be late.

explanationZhTw: `hostel` 指青年旅館或宿舍式住宿，比一般的 `hotel` 更簡單、便宜。
generatedExample: We stayed in a hostel near the station.

explanationZhTw: `get in` 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「進入、到達」；在這句裡是指要更早進去。
generatedExample: You need a code to get in after midnight.

explanationZhTw: `filthy brute` 是帶有強烈侮辱意味的說法，`brute` 在這裡不是「野獸」本身，而是罵人粗魯、沒教養。
generatedExample: He called the man a filthy brute after the argument.

explanationZhTw: 「twisted herself over」在這裡是指她把身體整個翻轉過來，不是字面上「扭曲」。這種寫法常用來描述人快速、用力地轉身或翻身。
generatedExample: She twisted herself over to face the window and listened to the rain.

explanationZhTw: 「seized」在這裡是「迅速抓起、拿起」的意思，帶有用力、突然的感覺，比一般的「take」更強烈。
generatedExample: He seized his coat and rushed out of the house.

explanationZhTw: 「hurtling」表示「高速地衝去、飛去」，常暗示動作又快又猛。這裡是在形容鞋子被用力丟出去後快速飛向角落。
generatedExample: The bicycle came hurtling down the hill.

explanationZhTw: 「boyish jerk」在這裡不是指「男孩式的猛拉」這麼字面，而是形容動作粗魯、帶點孩子氣的突然一甩。整個片語描述的是一種短促、有力的動作。
generatedExample: With a boyish jerk of his shoulders, he shrugged off the criticism.

explanationZhTw: 「fling」在這裡是「猛地投擲、甩出去」的意思，語氣比一般的「throw」更急、更有力量。
generatedExample: She flung her bag onto the chair and sat down.

explanationZhTw: 「in surprise」表示「驚訝地、帶著詫異的語氣」。這裡不是指「驚喜」，而是說話時的反應方式。
generatedExample: She looked at him in surprise when he mentioned her name.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `beastly` 不是字面上的「像野獸」，而是表示「很討厭的、惡劣的、難看的」；用來加強說話者對那隻老鼠的厭惡感。
generatedExample: The beastly weather ruined our picnic.

explanationZhTw: `wainscoting` 指牆壁下半部的木板護牆板或裝飾板，是偏建築／室內裝修的詞，日常對話不算常見。
generatedExample: The old house still has dark wooden wainscoting in the hallway.

explanationZhTw: `gave him a good fright` 是固定說法，意思是「把他嚇了一大跳／嚇得不輕」。
generatedExample: The sudden bang gave me a good fright.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指動物「老鼠」，而是表示懊惱、失望或驚訝的感嘆詞，類似「可惡！」、「糟了！」。
generatedExample: "Rats! I left my keys at home," she said.

explanationZhTw: 指「低聲說；喃喃地說」，聲音很小，通常別人不容易聽清楚。
generatedExample: He murmured an apology and walked away.

explanationZhTw: 片語，表示「到處都是、滿地都是」；這裡不是字面上的「在各處」。
generatedExample: Bugs were all over the place after the rain.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `got` 不是「拿到」，而是口語的「有、存在著」。
generatedExample: We've got a small garden behind the house.

explanationZhTw: 表示「擠滿了、充斥著」；常用來形容大量會動的東西，特別是昆蟲。
generatedExample: The park was swarming with tourists in summer.

explanationZhTw: 這是 `dare not` 的縮寫，意思是「不敢」；屬於較舊或文學感較強的用法。
generatedExample: He daren't speak up in the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般字面義，而是帶鄙視語氣，指那些令人討厭的動物。
generatedExample: Those brutes kept scratching at the door all night.

explanationZhTw: 「go on」在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「繼續、說下去」。在這句話中，Winston 要對方停止或不要繼續。
generatedExample: Please go on with your story; I was listening.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是親暱稱呼，意思接近「親愛的／最親愛的」，不是字面上的「最珍貴的」。
generatedExample: Dearest, are you coming home soon?

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `gone` 不是「走了」，而是表示「變得」；整個片語是「臉色變得很蒼白」。
generatedExample: After hearing the news, he went quite pale.

explanationZhTw: 這是固定說法，用來問「怎麼了？」或「發生什麼事了？」
generatedExample: You look worried. What's the matter?

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「感到不舒服／想吐」的意思，屬於常見但需靠上下文判斷的用法。
generatedExample: The smell of the food makes me feel sick.

explanationZhTw: 這是一種感嘆式說法，意思是「在所有可怕的事物當中，這個最糟」；用來強調接下來提到的東西非常令人恐懼。
generatedExample: Of all surprises in the world, a surprise exam on Monday morning!

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `rat` 是名詞，指「老鼠」。在這個語境中，它不只是一般動物，也帶有強烈的恐懼與厭惡感。
generatedExample: There was a rat in the kitchen, and everyone backed away immediately.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `wind/wound ... round` 是「纏繞、盤繞」；指她用四肢環抱住他，不是「受傷」的 wound。
generatedExample: She wound the scarf round her neck before going outside.

explanationZhTw: `reassure` 是「使安心、安撫、讓人放心」。
generatedExample: I called to reassure him that the results would be fine.

explanationZhTw: `unendurable` 表示「無法忍受的、難以承受的」。
generatedExample: The heat in the tiny room was unendurable after noon.

explanationZhTw: `self-deception` 是「自我欺騙」；明知真相，卻騙自己不去面對。
generatedExample: Saying I was too busy was just self-deception.

explanationZhTw: 這裡 `deadly` 不是「致命的」，而是加強語氣，表示「極其費力的、非常艱難的」。
generatedExample: It took a deadly effort to keep climbing in the cold wind.

explanationZhTw: `into the open` 是慣用說法，表示「公開出來、攤到檯面上、揭露出來」。
generatedExample: The investigation brought the corruption into the open.

explanationZhTw: `cut someone short` 表示「打斷某人，不讓對方把話說完」。
generatedExample: He cut her short and asked for the main point right away.

explanationZhTw: 表示「對不起／抱歉」，是很常見的基本用語。這裡只是禮貌地先道歉或緩和語氣。
generatedExample: I'm sorry, I forgot to call you earlier.

explanationZhTw: 表示「就只是這樣／僅此而已」，用來強調沒有別的原因或補充說明。
generatedExample: I was tired, that's all.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `brutes` 不是單純指「野獸」，而是帶有貶抑意味地指「粗魯、沒教養的人」；`filthy` 也加強了侮辱語氣，整個片語是強烈的罵人說法。
generatedExample: The landlord complained that the noisy visitors were filthy brutes.

explanationZhTw: `stuff` 這裡不是名詞「東西」，而是動詞「把...塞滿、填住」；`stuff the hole` 就是把洞先臨時塞起來。
generatedExample: She stuffed the crack with paper to stop the cold air.

explanationZhTw: `sacking` 指粗麻布、麻袋布之類的粗糙布料，常用來包裹、遮蓋或填塞。
generatedExample: They covered the potatoes with sacking to keep them dry.

explanationZhTw: `bung it up` 是口語用法，意思是把某個洞、裂縫或開口粗略地堵住、封起來。
generatedExample: We can bung it up with tape until we get a better repair.

explanationZhTw: 「為自己感到羞愧／難為情」。這裡表示他因剛才的事而有點內疚。
generatedExample: He felt ashamed of himself after forgetting their anniversary.

explanationZhTw: 「起床；下床」。`get out of bed` 是很常見的片語動詞。
generatedExample: I got out of bed early to catch the first train.

explanationZhTw: 「連身工作服；吊帶工作褲」。通常指工作時穿的衣服。
generatedExample: He wore overalls while painting the fence.

explanationZhTw: 「愛打聽的；好奇的」。這裡有點偏向『會想追問、探看』的意思。
generatedExample: The reporter was inquisitive and asked several sharp questions.

explanationZhTw: 「絲滑的質地／口感」。這裡形容咖啡喝起來很順口、很細緻。
generatedExample: The soup had a silky texture after I blended it longer.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般形容詞「過甜的」，而是指「糖」或「甜味劑」這類東西；屬於語境中的特殊用法。
generatedExample: The drink tasted strange because it was full of saccharine.

explanationZhTw: 「四處走動；閒晃」。這裡表示 Julia 在房間裡不特定地走來走去。
generatedExample: We wandered about the town after dinner.

explanationZhTw: 「重重地坐下；一屁股坐下」。`plump down` 帶有動作俐落、稍微用力的感覺。
generatedExample: She plumped herself down on the sofa and sighed.

explanationZhTw: 「破舊的；磨損嚴重的」。這裡形容那張扶手椅外觀很舊、很破。
generatedExample: The ragged curtains fluttered in the wind.

explanationZhTw: 「帶著寬容的好笑／寬厚的玩味」。表示不是生氣，而是有點覺得可笑但仍能包容。
generatedExample: He watched the children with tolerant amusement.

explanationZhTw: 「著迷的；被吸引住的」。這裡表示他每次都被那個物件深深吸引。
generatedExample: I was fascinated by the old maps in the archive.

explanationZhTw: 這是語境中的特殊形容，指玻璃看起來像雨水一樣清澈、柔和、帶點濕潤感。
generatedExample: The stone had a rainwatery appearance after the shower.

explanationZhTw: 「put to any use」是指「被拿來使用／有實際用途」，這裡是否定用法，表示它從來沒有被實際用過。
generatedExample: This old tool was never put to any use after we bought the new one.

explanationZhTw: 「chunk」原本是「一大塊、一部分」，這裡在「chunk of history」中表示「一小段歷史／過去的一部分」。
generatedExample: The diary is a little chunk of family history.

explanationZhTw: 「alter」是「改變、修改」；在這裡指把東西改動成別的樣子，帶有「被人刻意修正」的意思。
generatedExample: She had to alter the dress so it fit better.

explanationZhTw: 「message」在這裡不是一般的「訊息」，而是指「留下來的線索、歷史痕跡」；整句是在說這東西像是一百年前留下的訊號。
generatedExample: The ruins are like a message from a lost civilization.

explanationZhTw: 「點頭示意」；這裡表示她用點頭來指向或回應那幅圖像。
generatedExample: She nodded to show that she agreed.

explanationZhTw: 「雕刻版畫；刻印圖」；指刻在木、金屬等材料上的圖像或版畫。
generatedExample: The museum has an old engraving of the city.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是在問那幅雕刻是否「已經有一百年歷史」，不是在問人的年齡。
generatedExample: This house is about two hundred years old.

explanationZhTw: 表示「我想／我猜／大概是」；用來提出不太確定但合理的推測。
generatedExample: I dare say he will arrive before noon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「講不出來」，而是「無法判斷／說不準」的意思。
generatedExample: From the photo alone, I can't tell who it is.

explanationZhTw: 表示「如今／現在」，常用來對比過去與現在。
generatedExample: People rely on phones for maps nowadays.

explanationZhTw: `go over` 在這裡是「走過去、走到那邊」的意思，不是「仔細檢查」。
generatedExample: She went over to the door and opened it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `brute` 不是動物，而是罵人用語，指粗魯、殘暴或令人討厭的人。
generatedExample: That brute kept shouting at everyone in the room.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，字面上是「把鼻子伸出來」；在這裡指某個東西或人的鼻子從邊緣凸出來，語氣帶有戲謔或不滿。
generatedExample: He stuck his nose out of the tent to see who was outside.

explanationZhTw: `wainscoting` 指牆面下半部的護壁板或木牆裙，是室內裝潢用語。
generatedExample: The old dining room had dark wainscoting along the walls.

explanationZhTw: 表示「以前是／曾經是」，暗示現在已經不是那樣了。
generatedExample: This building used to be a cinema.

explanationZhTw: 指「片段、碎片」；這裡是指一小段被想起來的歌謠。
generatedExample: I only remember a fragment of the speech.

explanationZhTw: 表示「突然想起來、浮現在腦中」。
generatedExample: Her warning came back into my head when I saw the sign.

explanationZhTw: 表示帶著「半懷舊、略帶感傷」的語氣。
generatedExample: He smiled half-nostalgically at the old photo.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `capped` 不是字面上的「戴帽子」；是指她接著說了一句話，把前面的對話收尾，形成一個出乎意料的回應。
generatedExample: She made a cutting remark, and he capped the line with a quiet laugh.

explanationZhTw: 「owe」在這裡是「欠（錢）」；整句是在說某人還欠我錢。
generatedExample: I owe my brother ten dollars.

explanationZhTw: 「farthing」是英國舊制貨幣單位，金額非常小；這裡帶有古老、童謠式的語氣。
generatedExample: He found two old farthings in a drawer.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「say」不是一般的「說」，而是把鐘聲擬人化，表示鐘聲在「唱出／報出」這句話。
generatedExample: The bells seemed to say the town was waking up.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `go on` 不是「走上去」，而是表示「繼續、接下去」。
generatedExample: The story goes on for another chapter.

explanationZhTw: `end up` 表示「最後變成、結果是」，強調事情的最終結果。
generatedExample: If we leave now, we might end up getting there early.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `chopper` 不是日常最常見的用法，而是指「砍頭用的刀斧／斬首工具」之類的東西。
generatedExample: In the old story, the chopper looked like a huge axe.

explanationZhTw: 指「暗號、口令」；這裡不是一般的「簽名」，而是用來辨認身分或對應回應的秘密口令。
generatedExample: The guard asked for the countersign before opening the gate.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，指「設法從記憶中挖出、回想出來」；這裡是比喻用法，不是真的挖土。
generatedExample: After a few questions, she dug out the name from her memory.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「被適當引導、提示後才想得起來」；語氣比一般的「提醒」更偏向用線索刺激回想。
generatedExample: He remembered the answer only when the teacher prompted him.

explanationZhTw: `taught` 是 `teach` 的過去式，意思是「教導、傳授」。這裡是在問「是誰教你這個的？」
generatedExample: My grandmother taught me how to make this soup.

explanationZhTw: 表示過去的習慣或常態，這裡是「以前常常對我說」。
generatedExample: My father used to read me a story every night.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是字面上的「蒸發」，而是指被抹除、處決，或被政權弄得消失無蹤。
generatedExample: The witness was vaporized after he refused to cooperate.

explanationZhTw: 表示「無論如何／總之」，用來把話題拉回重點或修正前一句。
generatedExample: It was late, but at any rate we reached the station safely.

explanationZhTw: 表示說話離題、不連貫地補充一句；這裡帶有漫不經心、跳題的語氣。
generatedExample: He answered inconsequently, as if he were thinking about something else.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `common` 表示「常見的、普遍的」，不是「普通得沒特色」的評價。
generatedExample: Wire phones were still common in my grandparents' house when I was a child.

explanationZhTw: `sour` 在這裡是形容味道「很酸」；也常用來表示令人不舒服或帶刺的語氣。
generatedExample: The oranges were too sour to eat without sugar.

explanationZhTw: 這個習語表示某種感覺或聲音讓人非常不舒服、甚至有點牙齒發酸的反應。
generatedExample: That high-pitched noise set my teeth on edge.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `bugs` 不是「蟲子」，而是指偷偷裝在牆後或物件裡的竊聽器、監聽裝置；屬於情境下的特殊用法。
generatedExample: The office was searched for bugs before the meeting started.

explanationZhTw: `take it down` 是常見片語動詞，意思是把某物從原本的位置移走、拆下來。
generatedExample: We should take the old poster down this afternoon.

explanationZhTw: `wash ... off` 表示把表面的東西洗掉、沖掉；這裡是把身上的油漆洗掉。
generatedExample: It took me ten minutes to wash the mud off my shoes.

explanationZhTw: 這是固定感嘆語，表示「真煩／真無聊」；不是在字面上說某個東西本身「很無聊」，而是在表達對情況的不耐煩。
generatedExample: We had to wait for an hour, which was a bore.

explanationZhTw: 指壓紙器，也就是用來壓住紙張的小擺件；這裡是具體物品，不是「重量」的意思。
generatedExample: She picked up the glass paperweight and set it on the letters.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 interior 指「內部、裡面」，特別是玻璃的內部空間，而不是建築物的內部。
generatedExample: The interior of the shell looked smooth and pearly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 transparent 是「透明的、幾乎看得穿」；用來強調玻璃看起來很深卻仍能透視。
generatedExample: The water was so transparent that we could see the rocks below.

explanationZhTw: surface 指「表面」；這裡是玻璃外層，帶有把整個小世界包在裡面的想像。
generatedExample: Dust gathered on the surface of the table.

explanationZhTw: enclosing 在這裡是「包圍、圈住」的意思，表示玻璃像天空一樣把小世界罩住。
generatedExample: The fence was enclosing a small garden behind the house.

explanationZhTw: eternity 是「永恆、無盡的時間」；這裡強調畫面像被固定在永遠不變的狀態中。
generatedExample: For a moment, the silence felt like eternity.
